arts.ttl: 'Let's have a right go' - Middlesbrough's rival bosses on the Championship play-off race - Teesside Live
arts.sum: “We’ve still got really important games coming up and I think now we just need to put all our attention and try to win the game against Blackburn. There will be just two games and six points left to play for by that point, meaning this weekend could quite literally make or break Boro’s season.

arts.ttl: James Wilson and fellow Scotland starlet pen new Hearts contracts
arts.sum: Jambos boss Critchley said: “It’s great news that both James and Adam have committed their futures to the club. Rising Scotland stars James Wilson and Adam Forrester have both signed new contracts with Hearts .

arts.ttl: Plymouth Argyle and Sheffield United charged following post-match fracas
arts.sum: Plymouth Argyle and Sheffield United are charged with misconduct after a fracas following their Championship game on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Championship: Predictions for all 12 games on Friday
arts.sum: The Easter weekend double header in the Championship usually has major repercussions at both ends of the table and Tom McGarry is on hand to provide a score prediction for all 12 games in the second tier on Good Friday.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick makes 'miracle' point and issues defiant 'game on' Middlesbrough play-off plea
arts.sum: They now face two crucial games over the Easter weekend, with Plymouth Argyle visiting the Riverside on Good Friday, before a trip to Sheffield Wednesday on Easter Monday. Michael Carrick insists Middlesbrough don’t need an Easter miracle to qualify for this season’s play-offs, but understands this weekend could be vital in shaping the final league table.

arts.ttl: What is Nikola Katic’s estimated transfer value amid permanent Plymouth Argyle transfer questions?
arts.sum: Plymouth Argyle signed centre-back Nikola Katic in the winter transfer window and the Bosnian has been a hit… The post What is Nikola Katic’s estimated transfer value amid permanent Plymouth Argyle transfer questions? appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Boro frustrations post-January, damp squib fear and changes for Michael Carrick
arts.sum: They did very well in the season half against Oxford, were superb at Blackburn and battled hard to try to get back into the game after the early setback against Leeds. The deliberate policy to try to further strengthen in January, even though there was little chance of keeping Latte Lath, should have given the season a fresh kick-start.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick explains Middlesbrough substutions policy with 'different factors' claim
arts.sum: Discussing it immediately after the game, Carrick played down the influence of the Millwall changes and pointed out that Riley McGree and Marcus Forss were ready to come on as the Lions scored their all-important goal in a damaging 1-0 defeat for Boro. Not for the first time, the loss for Boro came as an opposition manager made changes to positively influence the game while Carrick didn't react until it was ultimately too late.
